summary: Register the corpus-wide frontier research ROI outlook, its audited corpus census, and its ranked Arithmetic Horizon Variation programme.

artifacts.title: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook
artifacts.summary: A risk-adjusted corpus-wide evaluation of prospective reach, publication leverage, and frontier research targets, prioritizing an exact parity/intersection theorem, horizon-cover Gauss-Manin transport, collision/vanishing-cycle geometry, and a conditional curvature-discriminant bridge.

claims.statement: At DAG revision sha256:00ef143b0e00f29f8a8f97ee4583907262db1c829ff6ba8c236cb71a3719fec1, the corpus contains 2294 nodes, 4311 edges, 57 gap nodes, 35 tracked frontier items, 19 ghost deliverables, and no DAG validation errors or warnings.
hypotheses: Counts are tied to the declared canonical DAG revision.
The 508-row paper-library ledger is treated as a snapshot and the later admitted PAP-0509 is counted separately for an effective 509-artifact corpus.
claims.scope: Corpus census used by the research ROI analysis.

claims.statement: Under the report's explicit weighted-benefit, cost, and risk model, the Kummer parity/intersection target has the highest frontier priority, followed by horizon Gauss-Manin transport, while immediate paper integration has the highest support priority.
hypotheses: Use the six benefit dimensions and penalty formula stated in Section 6.
Scores are prioritization judgments rather than theorem probabilities or citation forecasts.
claims.scope: Risk-adjusted ordering of the research targets evaluated in the report.

relations.basis: The report positions period transport and the integral-lattice gaps as the highest-leverage interface to the Galois arm.
The report defines a staged programme from horizon-cover monodromy through Kummer parity, vanishing cycles, and curvature comparison.
The released all-k weighted theorem is identified as the strongest reusable existing bridgehead.
The highest-ranked frontier target directly addresses the Smith-form and integral-intersection gap.
The parity/intersection campaign is designed to turn concrete Kummer rank into a structural all-k criterion.
Gauss-Manin and vanishing-cycle targets provide an exact route to generic complex braid and wall transport.
The report isolates collision-stratum realization as a high-upside vanishing-cycle and quiver target with explicit prerequisites.
The curvature-discriminant target is explicitly constrained by the mixed front-face coefficient gap and its known correctness boundary.
The report defers categorical unification until the period and horizon arms produce a concrete reduced common skeleton.

declared_impacts.note: Prioritize an exact parity/intersection benchmark as the first frontier campaign.
Attack all-k Kummer closure through an equivariant lattice criterion before another brute-force crown computation.
Sequence complex braid transport after the lattice and one-parameter Gauss-Manin gates.
Use counterexample-first discriminant/curvature tests to isolate the minimal sufficient local data.

llm.one_sentence_purpose: Rank the corpus's best support and frontier investments and define a falsifiable programme connecting horizon-cover Galois data to lattices, periods, collision geometry, curvature, and eventually a specified string model.
retrieval_questions: Which Cella research target has the strongest risk-adjusted frontier ROI?
How should the Galois horizon breakthrough be connected to period geometry and string theory?
What exact theorem should be attempted before claiming a black-hole charge-lattice interpretation?
Which current gaps can one parity/intersection theorem advance?
What are the stop/go gates for the Arithmetic Horizon Variation programme?

upsert_nodes.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook
upsert_nodes.summary: Risk-adjusted corpus-wide outlook ranking a parity/intersection theorem and horizon Gauss-Manin transport as the strongest frontier investments, with collision, curvature, string, support, falsification, and publication programmes.

retrieval_questions: What is the strongest next frontier target in the Cella corpus?
How should horizon Galois theory be bridged to periods, collision geometry, and black-hole physics?
Which bridge campaigns have finite falsifiers and the highest downstream leverage?

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook supplements Paper III
upsert_edges.basis: Ranks period transport and integral lattices as the highest-leverage interface to the Galois arm.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook supplements Paper IV
upsert_edges.basis: Defines the staged Arithmetic Horizon Variation programme extending Paper IV's monodromy and Kummer results.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook cites the weighted multiquadratic monodromy paper
upsert_edges.basis: Uses the all-k weighted theorem as the general algebraic bridgehead for the programme.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook concerns gap III6
upsert_edges.basis: Makes the integral-intersection and Smith-form gap the first exact frontier campaign.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook concerns gap IV3
upsert_edges.basis: Targets all-k Kummer closure through an equivariant parity/intersection criterion.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook concerns gap IV7
upsert_edges.basis: Sequences complex braid transport after lattice and Gauss-Manin construction.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook concerns collision realization
upsert_edges.basis: Defines a vanishing-cycle and quiver realization campaign for higher collision strata.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook concerns gap II2
upsert_edges.basis: Places mixed front-face data inside a counterexample-first curvature-discriminant programme.

upsert_edges.label: Cella Frontier Research ROI Outlook concerns gap V2
upsert_edges.basis: Defers categorical unification until a concrete period/horizon reduced skeleton exists.

declared_impacts.note: Promote exact parity/intersection benchmarks to the first frontier campaign.
Use a structural equivariant-lattice route to all-k Kummer closure.
Require the lattice and one-parameter Gauss-Manin gates before the generic braid campaign.
Run exact same-inertia/different-jet falsifiers before seeking a universal curvature formula.

extensions.evaluation_basis: live canonical DAG census plus primary-literature mechanism scan
